title: Data Binding
content:
### What is Data Binding? Data binding synchronizes data between the component class (model) and the template (view). Angular provides four forms of data binding with a consistent syntax. ### 1. Interpolation ‚Äî `{{ }}` Displays component property values in the template: ```html

{{ title }}

{{ user.name }} is {{ user.age }} years old.

{{ 2 + 2 }}

{{ greet() }}

``` Interpolation evaluates expressions and converts the result to a string. ### 2. Property Binding ‚Äî `[ ]` Sets an element property or directive input to a component expression: ```html
Active div
Colored text
``` Property binding flows **from component ‚Üí template**. ### 3. Event Binding ‚Äî `( )` Listens to DOM events and calls component methods: ```html
...
``` Event binding flows **from template ‚Üí component**. ### 4. Two-Way Binding ‚Äî `[( )]` The **banana-in-a-box** syntax combines property and event binding: ```html ``` Two-way binding flows **both ways** ‚Äî component ‚Üî template. ### @Input() and @Output() **`@Input()`** ‚Äî Allows parent components to pass data to a child: ```typescript // child.component.ts @Input() user: User; @Input({ required: true }) title: string; ``` ```html ``` **`@Output()`** ‚Äî Allows child components to emit events to parents: ```typescript // child.component.ts @Output() userSaved = new EventEmitter(); save() { this.userSaved.emit(this.user); } ``` ```html ```

quiz.question: What syntax does Angular use for two-way data binding?
Which decorator allows a parent component to pass data to a child component?
